name: Iris Ohyama 17L Compact Microwave
pros: Compact 17L size perfect for single apartments and dorm rooms
Reliable Iris Ohyama brand with straightforward single-function design
cons: Only 302 reviews suggest limited market penetration compared to competitors
Basic turntable model offers no inverter or sensor cooking technology
shortDescription: Practical compact microwave for one-person households at reasonable price.
review: Iris Ohyama's small microwave is honest and functional—good for single expats on a budget. It heats food reliably but lacks the smart features (inverter, sensor) of pricier models. Japanese instructions come standard; English manual may need searching. At ¥7,980, it's fair value but not exceptional.
